query_normalized: evolutionary pressure on alternative splicing in primates biology
rationale: This paper satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) because it investigates the causal relationship between recombination rate and exon density (a proxy for splicing/exon structure) in the human genome, which is a core primate model for studying evolutionary pressures on genome architecture. Although it does not explicitly mention "alternative splicing" or "primates" broadly, it measures the underlying mechanism (recombination-driven evolution of exon density) in the specific empirical populati
